title: NavigationTransition
description: A directed edge in the navigation graph representing an authorized route change from a source route to a destination route, triggered by a specific mechanism (user click, programmatic dispatch, timeout, browser-back, or redirect). Models the actual paths users traverse between states in the application — distinct from the routes themselves — enabling analysis of navigation flows, dead-end detection, and trigger-based transition policies.

trigger.description: Mechanism that initiates the route transition: user-click (explicit user interaction), programmatic (code-driven), timeout (time-based), browser-back (browser history navigation), redirect (automatic server/guard redirect).
